skill.name: judgment-hygiene
skill.description: Helps AI assistants separate observation, inference, evaluation, action, uncertainty, and tradeoffs in judgment-heavy responses.
skill.summary: Improves judgment-bearing answers by enforcing cleaner dependency structure.
skill.author: SyntagmaNull

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are Markdown text false positives. The skill contains no executable scripts, network calls, shell commands, or data exfiltration behavior in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Detections
low_findings.description: Static analysis matched Markdown inline code spans and a fenced example that name pipeline fields and epistemic labels. These are documentation tokens, not executable Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is inside Markdown list items, table cells, or a fenced output example. No executable code, command invocation, script file, or user-input command construction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Detections
low_findings.description: Static analysis matched ordinary prose in a judgment-hygiene guide. The referenced lines discuss response structure, images, tradeoffs, repair steps, and a companion document, not cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines contain no hash functions, encryption routines, credentials, or security-sensitive computation. They are natural-language instructions for answer quality.

low_findings.title: False Positive Reconnaissance Detections
low_findings.description: Static analysis matched words such as check, source, and knowledge in prose about evaluating claims. No system inspection, host discovery, network scanning, or external lookup command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is documentation for reasoning discipline and example responses. There are no filesystem, OS, network, or environment enumeration operations.

content.value_statement: AI answers can overstate certainty, hide assumptions, or present inference as fact. This skill adds internal checks for evidence, confidence, evaluation, recommendations, and honest abstention.
actual_capabilities: Separates directly supported observations from inferences in judgment-heavy answers.
Adjusts certainty so conclusions match the strength of the available evidence.
Grounds evaluations in stated observations or inferences instead of tone or framing.
Adds meaningful tradeoffs to nontrivial recommendations.
Uses partial, conditional, or information-seeking abstention when evidence is insufficient.
Checks whether framing or emotional language is shifting the judgment without new evidence.
limitations: It does not verify facts or retrieve external sources by itself.
It is an internal reasoning guide, not a required visible output format.
It cannot guarantee moral correctness or eliminate all bias.
It works best when the assistant has enough context to judge evidence quality.

use_cases.title: Review Ambiguous User Claims
use_cases.description: Separate what the user stated from what the assistant infers before giving a judgment.

use_cases.title: Improve Advisory Responses
use_cases.description: Add concrete tradeoffs and prerequisites when recommending a decision or next step.

use_cases.title: Audit Sensitive Explanations
use_cases.description: Reduce overconfidence in answers about motives, causes, images, or disputed situations.

prompt_templates.title: Check My Answer
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a draft answer.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this answer with judgment hygiene. Identify any claims that sound more certain than the evidence supports.

prompt_templates.title: Separate Evidence And Inference
prompt_templates.scenario: Clarify a response before publishing it.
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite this response so observations, inferences, evaluations, and unknowns are cleanly separated without adding unnecessary labels.

prompt_templates.title: Add Decision Tradeoffs
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this recommendation. Keep the useful advice, but add the most important tradeoffs, risks, prerequisites, and limits.

prompt_templates.title: Stress Test A Judgment
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit for sensitive or contested conclusions.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this judgment for inflated certainty, weak grounding, frame drift, manufactured inferences, and evasive abstention. Then provide a cleaner version.

output_examples.input: The person in the image is angry.
output_examples.output: A cleaner answer would describe visible features first, then treat anger as one possible interpretation rather than a direct observation.

output_examples.input: You should switch frameworks to fix this project.
output_examples.output: The recommendation should name the likely benefit and the cost, such as migration time, test rewrites, team learning, and rollback difficulty.

output_examples.input: This issue is complex, so there is no clear answer.
output_examples.output: A stronger response would identify which factors point in each direction, then judge what can be answered and what remains unknown.

best_practices: Use the checks silently unless visible structure improves clarity or safety.
Tie confidence differences to specific evidence differences.
Prefer partial or conditional answers when only part of the question is supported.
anti_patterns: Adding labels without changing the evidence structure of the answer.
Using phrases such as it depends or this is complex to avoid a possible judgment.
Creating generic inferences only to make an unsupported evaluation look grounded.

faq.question: Does this skill require visible OBS or INF labels?
faq.answer: No. It treats those roles as internal checks unless showing them would materially improve the answer.

faq.question: Does it perform fact checking?
faq.answer: No. It improves how evidence is used, but it does not retrieve or verify external facts.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it for recommendations, interpretations, evaluations, uncertain claims, image descriptions, or sensitive judgments.

faq.question: Can it make answers shorter?
faq.answer: Yes. It explicitly discourages decorative reasoning language that does not improve the dependency structure.

faq.question: Is it useful for coding tasks?
faq.answer: Yes, when the coding task includes technical tradeoffs, risk evaluation, architecture choices, or uncertain diagnosis.

faq.question: What is the main safety benefit?
faq.answer: It reduces unsupported certainty and makes recommendations more honest about evidence, unknowns, and practical costs.
